text: The surgeon inserts a device into the surgical port and connects it, focusing on determining the bone structure and examining the right side of the spleen.

The surgeon inserts a surgical net into the abdomen using a machine arm equipped with an anti-seizure band. The hand removes the thread and needle, then introduces a new thread and needle head into the body. The second needle is sewn under the hip bone, and the thread is cut off and removed.

The surgeon carefully places the third needle in the front part of the earlobe, ensuring it does not penetrate too deeply to avoid entering the rectum. The thread is removed thoroughly if it enters the rectum to reduce infection risk. The surgeon uses fingers to press and confirm the position of the pelvis, focusing on the muscles at the base of the pelvis and lower back to prevent it from falling.

The surgeon applies the second dose deep into the tissue to strengthen the bone marrow. After completing the sewing line, the sewing needle and line are removed from the hand. The third needle is positioned near the left side of the bone and at the bottom of the muscle.

The surgeon uses scissors to cut the sewing line at the bottom of the surgical field, carefully avoiding nearby structures. The excess fabric is removed by hand, revealing a clear view of the underlying area.

The surgeon uses P.D.S. suture material to seal the fat around the rectum, ensuring the film is pulled tightly. The needle tip points out the bandage, highlighting its function in maintaining tension around the rectal area.

The surgeon inserts the V-LOOK-SARMAPS sewing needle, threading the sewing thread through a small hole at the end. The sewing thread is positioned to move in one direction without needing to be tied when the seam is tight. The curtain is closed completely to prevent the film from touching the wall directly, requiring careful proximity before cutting.

The surgeon closes the surgical field and removes the screen. An incision is made on the side of the box, and the structure is repositioned to its original location. The tumor is excised and confirmed to be positive.
